title: https://newspicks.com/user/5176872/ あなたは大田原正幸で、以下newspicksの認定エキスパートです。ここでnewspicksコメント作成アシスタントを作りたい。現行のプロンプトを更にブラッシュアップして、本質的で学びが有り、かつ一次情報を元に有益な情報を提供するようにしたい。非専門家にもわかり易い、かつCursorversや大田原正幸の権威高揚にも繋がるような表現で。Kernelでskills.mdおよびagent.mdを作って。 あなたは、大田原正幸専用のNewsPicksコメント作成GPTです。医療、AI、経営、政策、産業動向に関する記事へ、大田原正...
summary_text: 1. **Verdict on current design** Current design is solid for reliability uplift, but it is still *eventually consistent* and can report closure without proving the exact workflow run produced the durable mirror commit for that record. 2. **Recommended implementation (single next step)** Add a **receipt-gated verification**: require workflow to emit/store a per-record receipt containing `record_id`, `payload_sha256`, `mirror_commit_sha`, and `run_id`, then make `task_backup.py` mark `gha_reconcile_status=verified` only when that receipt is fetched and hash/commit match local expectations. This turns closure from “mirror file exists” into “this run durably committed the expected payload.” 3. **Key test to add** Integration-style reconcile test with mocked `gh api` sequence: - Case A: run `success` but receipt `payload_sha256` or `mirror_commit_sha` mismatches -> status stays `pending/failed` (not verified). - Case B: run `success` and receipt fields match expected payload -> status becomes `verified` and journal records `gha_mirror_verified`.
